criteria.description: A pure module cost_audit.py exists at the repo root and imports successfully with all provider API keys unset (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/DEEPGRAM_API_KEY/CARTESIA_API_KEY unset) and with no network access, importing only Python stdlib + pathlib. It must not import app, bot, pipecat, or any third-party package.
criteria.verifyBy: In a subprocess with those env vars removed, run `python -c 'import cost_audit'`; assert exit code 0. Grep the module source to confirm no `import bot`, `import app`, or `import pipecat` (and no third-party import) statements.

criteria.description: cost_audit.py defines, as module-level numeric constants, the full verbatim pricing set used by the logger: PRICE_ANTHROPIC_INPUT_PER_MTOK, PRICE_ANTHROPIC_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK, PRICE_ANTHROPIC_CACHE_READ_PER_MTOK, PRICE_ANTHROPIC_CACHE_WRITE_PER_MTOK, PRICE_ANTHROPIC_HAIKU_INPUT_PER_MTOK, PRICE_ANTHROPIC_HAIKU_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK, PRICE_DEEPGRAM_NOVA3_PER_MIN, and PRICE_CARTESIA_PER_CHAR (names may match bot.py's existing identifiers exactly). Since bot.py exists in the target repo, the constants are relocated into cost_audit and bot.py re-imports them with values unchanged (no logger behavior change).
criteria.verifyBy: Import cost_audit and assert each of the eight named constants exists and is a number (int/float). Import bot and assert every one of those constants exposed by bot equals the value in cost_audit (verbatim identity). If names differ, assert bot re-imports the same objects from cost_audit.

criteria.description: cost_audit exposes a pure helper that recomputes a session row's cost components and total from the row's own usage fields. LLM cost uses all four token classes at their four distinct per-MTok rates: llm_uncached_input_tokens×INPUT + llm_cache_read_tokens×CACHE_READ + llm_cache_write_tokens×CACHE_WRITE + llm_output_tokens×OUTPUT. STT cost uses the row's stored stt_minutes_billed × PRICE_DEEPGRAM_NOVA3_PER_MIN (the already-rounded minutes field named by the goal, not re-derived from session_duration_sec). TTS cost uses tts_chars × PRICE_CARTESIA_PER_CHAR. Post-session cost uses HAIKU rates: post_session_input_tokens×HAIKU_INPUT + post_session_output_tokens×HAIKU_OUTPUT. cost_total_usd is the sum of the four components.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests call the helper with fixtures where llm_cache_read_tokens and llm_cache_write_tokens are non-zero (so omitting them would give a different total) and assert each recomputed component (llm/stt/tts/post-session) and cost_total_usd match hand-computed expected values within the module tolerance from c7. At least one fixture uses non-zero post-session tokens to pin the Haiku-rate post-session math.

criteria.description: cost_audit exposes a pure helper that recomputes an artifact row's cost_usd from that row's own input/output token fields using the Haiku per-token rates (PRICE_ANTHROPIC_HAIKU_INPUT_PER_MTOK / PRICE_ANTHROPIC_HAIKU_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK), NOT the Sonnet input/output rates.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests call the artifact helper with a fixture whose expected cost under Haiku rates differs from what Sonnet rates would produce, and assert the recomputed cost_usd matches the Haiku-based hand-computed value within the c7 tolerance (and would fail under Sonnet rates).

criteria.description: The recomputation helpers are total and deterministic: they operate only on the row's own fields with no I/O, no network, and no reliance on API keys; identical inputs yield identical outputs.
criteria.verifyBy: Tests invoke the helpers repeatedly and assert identical results across calls; tests run and pass with API keys unset. No helper performs network or file access.

criteria.description: Hermetic unit tests exist (e.g. tests/test_cost_audit.py) that pin session and artifact recomputation against known usage->cost fixtures and prove cost_audit imports cleanly with keys unset and without importing bot/app.
criteria.verifyBy: Run pytest with API keys unset; assert the new cost_audit tests pass. Confirm at least one test asserts successful import under unset-keys conditions, at least one pins session recomputation (with non-zero cache and post-session tokens), and at least one pins artifact recomputation.

criteria.description: cost_audit defines a single module-level rounding tolerance constant, shared by helpers/tests (and available to a later CLI), set loose enough to absorb the logger's stored rounding — stored costs are round(x,4) and stt_minutes_billed is round(x,2) — so a correct row's recomputed value compares equal to its stored value without false mismatches. The tolerance must be at least 5e-5 (not the previously-proposed 1e-6).
criteria.verifyBy: Assert cost_audit exposes one module-level tolerance constant with value >= 5e-5, and that the recomputation tests use that same constant (not a hard-coded local epsilon) when comparing recomputed vs. expected/stored values.

criteria.description: All pre-existing tests in the target repo remain green after this change.
criteria.verifyBy: Run the full existing test suite before and after; assert no previously-passing test regresses.

scope.description: Read-only diagnosis foundation only: do not change how costs are logged. The only permitted edit to bot.py is re-importing the relocated pricing constants with values unchanged; no logger behavior changes.
Do not touch the voice pipeline, app.py routes, or study.html, nor any Pipecat/network/API-key-dependent code paths.

Keep changes confined to the new cost_audit module, its tests (and test fixtures/conftest), and the minimal bot.py constant re-import. Do not add third-party dependencies; the audit module is stdlib + pathlib only.

criteria.description: The pure-Python module `cost_audit.py` (stdlib + pathlib only; no pipecat/network/`import app`/`import bot` at module load) exposes a module-level log-path attribute pointing at the real cost-log.jsonl, monkeypatchable in tests following the sessions.py/grounding.py conftest pattern (reusing the existing `cost_audit_log_tmp` fixture).
criteria.verifyBy: Import cost_audit with API keys unset and pipecat absent; assert import succeeds. Grep to confirm no app/bot/pipecat/network imports and that a module-level log-path attribute exists. A test monkeypatches it to a fixture file and the audit reads from that file.

criteria.description: A pure audit entry function reads the module-level log path line by line and returns a structured summary result exposing: rows_read (total lines), rows_valid (rows with no error finding), per-category failure counts, and a findings list. Each finding exposes an enumerated stable category/kind field with value in {"malformed","cost_mismatch","orphan_artifact"}, a 1-based line number, and a human-readable reason string. The function does not import app/bot/pipecat and takes no network/API dependency.
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic test calls the audit function against a fixture jsonl and asserts the returned summary exposes rows_read, rows_valid, per-category counts, and a findings list, with each finding carrying category (from the enumerated set), a 1-based line number, and reason text. Test grades the return value directly (not stdout scraping).

criteria.description: Malformed detection is defined so that it partitions cleanly from cost_mismatch (c4) — no input class is claimed by both checks. A line is malformed iff: (a) it is not valid JSON, (b) it parses to a non-object (not a dict), or (c) it declares `kind` in {"session","artifact"} but lacks an identity/recompute key required to even classify and recompute the row — for a session row that key set is minimally `session_id`; for an artifact row it is `session_id`, `input_tokens`, and `output_tokens`. A missing or non-numeric stored `cost_*_usd` field (session) or stored `cost_usd` (artifact) is NOT malformed here — it is surfaced by c4 as a cost_mismatch (the recompute-vs-stored comparison treats an absent/non-numeric stored cost as a non-match). A row with NO `kind` field is NEVER malformed (it is classified legacy per c6). Malformed lines do not crash the audit; all subsequent lines are still processed and audited.
criteria.verifyBy: Fixtures include a non-JSON line and a `kind`-declaring row missing an identity key (e.g. a session row with no `session_id`). Test asserts each is flagged category "malformed" at its exact 1-based line number; that a row with no `kind` is NOT flagged malformed; that a `kind:session` row present with `session_id` but missing a stored `cost_*_usd` field is NOT flagged malformed but IS flagged cost_mismatch (c4), proving the c3/c4 partition; and that a valid row appearing AFTER a malformed line is still audited (proving no crash and continued processing).

criteria.description: Cost-mismatch detection pins recompute targets per row kind. Session rows check stored `cost_llm_usd`, `cost_stt_usd`, `cost_tts_usd`, `cost_post_session_usd`, and `cost_total_usd` against the values recomputed from the row's own usage fields via the session recompute helper; artifact rows check stored `cost_usd` against the artifact recompute helper (from input/output tokens). A stored value that disagrees beyond a small rounding tolerance — OR is absent/non-numeric while a recomputed value exists — is flagged category "cost_mismatch" with line number and a reason naming the offending cost field and stored-vs-recomputed values. A fully-correct multi-component session row (all five components present and consistent) and a within-tolerance difference produce NO mismatch finding.
criteria.verifyBy: Fixtures include a fully-correct session+artifact pair (no mismatch), a session row with one deliberately wrong stored cost component (flagged), a session row missing a stored cost field (flagged, per the c3/c4 partition), and a within-tolerance row (not flagged). Test asserts the wrong/missing rows' lines are flagged "cost_mismatch" naming the field, the correct multi-component row yields zero mismatch findings, and the within-tolerance row is clean.

criteria.description: Orphan detection uses `session_id` as the linkage key via a whole-file (two-phase) pass: an `artifact` row is orphan iff no `session` row anywhere in the same ledger — before OR after it — shares its `session_id`, flagged category "orphan_artifact" with line number and reason. A correctly paired session+artifact (matching `session_id`) yields no orphan finding regardless of ordering. A session row with no matching artifact is NORMAL and never flagged.
criteria.verifyBy: Fixture has (i) a correctly paired session+artifact with shared session_id, (ii) an artifact row that appears BEFORE its matching session row (forward reference), and (iii) a standalone artifact whose session_id matches no session row. Test asserts only the standalone artifact's line is flagged "orphan_artifact"; that the forward-referenced artifact is NOT flagged (proving the whole-file two-phase pass, not a backward-only scan); that the paired artifact is clean; and that a session row lacking an artifact produces no finding.

criteria.description: Legacy rows are tolerated: legacy is defined STRICTLY as absence of the `kind` field. Such rows are classified legacy and skipped by every error check (never malformed, cost_mismatch, or orphan). Missing `mode` is NOT a legacy marker — a current session row (declaring `kind`:"session") that omits `mode` is a normal session row and MUST still be cost-checked.
criteria.verifyBy: Fixture includes a pre-`kind` legacy row (no `kind` field) and a current session row that declares kind=session but omits `mode`. Test asserts the legacy row produces zero findings of any category, while the mode-less session row is still cost-checked (i.e. it participates in mismatch checking and is only clean if its stored costs match).

criteria.description: A hermetic characterization test suite covers all required fixture cases in one place — correct session+artifact pair, pre-`kind` legacy row, malformed line, orphan artifact, and cost-mismatch row — asserting each flagged line by 1-based line number and by enumerated category, and that clean/legacy rows produce no findings. The suite includes a malformed line immediately FOLLOWED by a valid mismatch or orphan row to prove line numbering stays correct (1-based, counting the malformed line). Tests pass with API keys unset and pipecat absent, and the full existing suite stays green.
criteria.verifyBy: Run the new test file with env keys unset and pipecat uninstalled; all new tests pass and the existing suite stays green. Inspect tests to confirm all five cases are asserted by line number and category, and that a malformed-then-valid ordering proves correct 1-based line numbering.

criteria.description: A CLI entry point (`python -m cost_audit` or equivalent `__main__`) runs the audit against the module-level log path and prints a summary: rows read, rows valid, per-check failure counts, and the offending line numbers each shown together with their category/reason (the DoD's 'list of offending line numbers with reasons'). The printed output is derived from the same structured summary that tests grade via the return value (stdout is a view, not the test hook).
criteria.verifyBy: Invoke the CLI against a small fixture log (module-level path monkeypatched or a temp path) and assert it exits cleanly and prints rows-read, rows-valid, and per-category counts, AND that for each finding the printed output contains its 1-based line number together with its category/reason (not merely aggregate counts). Confirm the underlying summary object (graded in c2) is what the CLI renders.

scope.description: Read-only diagnosis: do not change how costs are logged. No bot.py behavior changes this sprint (pricing-constant relocation/re-import was completed and frozen in sprint 0; do not re-litigate or re-modify it here).
Do not touch the voice pipeline, app.py routes, or study.html.
New code lives in the cost_audit module and its test/fixture files; do not rewrite unrelated modules (sessions.py, grounding.py, etc.) beyond reusing their conftest/log-path fixture pattern.
Internal-consistency auditing only (stored vs. recomputed from the row's own fields); comparison against real provider invoices is out of scope.
